title: Deterministic replay — multi-process causal ordering
purpose: Real workflows involve multiple processes communicating via messages. Deterministic replay of multi-process systems requires capturing the causal ordering of inter-process events (message sends, link signals, monitor notifications) so that replay reproduces the same interleaving.
task: Extend the replay infrastructure to capture and reproduce multi-process causal ordering.

requirements.title: Causal event ordering
requirements.spec: Record the causal order of inter-process events using vector clocks or sequence numbers.
acceptance: Each process maintains a logical clock (sequence number)
Message sends record sender clock and receiver clock at delivery
Replay driver enforces the same message delivery ordering
Test: two processes sending messages to each other — replay produces same interleaving

requirements.title: Scheduler determinism in replay
requirements.spec: The scheduler in replay mode uses the recorded event log to determine process scheduling order, not the normal run queue.
acceptance: Replay scheduler executes processes in the recorded order
Context switches happen at the same points as the recording
Test: replay with 3 processes produces identical scheduling order

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement distributed replay (across nodes) — single-node only
SHALL NOT implement partial ordering (Lamport clocks) — use total ordering from recording
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
